resources.string: Your private channel over the airwaves
Radium sends encrypted packets over the cellular signaling plane. We need SMS access to transmit and receive your messages securely — no internet required.
To receive incoming encrypted packets from other Radium users, we need permission to intercept binary SMS data on port 8080.
We monitor signal strength (RSRP, RSRQ, SNR) to ensure the physical link can support transmission before dispatching packets.
Cell tower identification (PCI, EARFCN) requires location access. This helps us gauge transmission viability and diagnose radio drops.
Select recipients from your contact list instead of typing numbers manually. We never upload your contacts — everything stays on-device.
No conversations yet.\nTap + to start one.
